---
title: "Europe Wildfire Season Tracker"
subtitle: "EFFIS rapid perimeters, updated weekly during fire season"
author: "Pierre Beaucoral"
date: today
---

This tracker follows the European wildfire season as it unfolds. It builds on
**EFFIS rapid perimeters** — the publicly mapped burn-scar polygons from the
Copernicus Emergency Management Service, typically covering fires of roughly
30–50 hectares and above. During the fire season (**June–September**) the site
refreshes **weekly**: a pipeline fetches the latest perimeters, snapshots them
under `DATA/snapshots/`, and the charts below re-render from the newest
snapshot. Burned areas are computed directly from polygon geometry in an
equal-area projection (EPSG:3035), and each perimeter is assigned to a country
by maximum overlap with national borders — the same method as the 2025 season
analysis this project grew from (it will be archived under *Seasons* soon).

::: {.callout-note title="2026 season — coming soon"}
The 2026 season data will load here once the automated fetch pipeline
(`scripts/fetch_effis.R`) lands. Until then, this page is a scaffold: no
numbers are shown because no data has been fetched yet. See the
[About](about.qmd) page for what to expect and the data caveats.
:::

## Season at a glance

*Placeholder — the envelope chart (2026 daily cumulative burned area against
the range of past seasons) will render here from the latest snapshot.*

```{r}
#| label: tracker-skeleton
#| eval: false
#| code-summary: "Show intended pipeline structure (not yet active)"

# ---- Intended structure of the live tracker (activates once data lands) ----

# 1) Source the shared helper library
source(file.path("R", "helpers.R"))  # parsing utilities, ha label formatter
source(file.path("R", "geo.R"))      # Europe polygons, perimeter reading, country tagging
source(file.path("R", "theme.R"))    # shared palette + theme_burns()
source(file.path("R", "cache.R"))    # disk cache for expensive geometry ops

# 2) Load the latest EFFIS snapshot written by scripts/fetch_effis.R
snapshot_dirs <- sort(list.dirs(file.path("DATA", "snapshots"), recursive = FALSE))
latest <- snapshot_dirs[length(snapshot_dirs)]

# 3) Read perimeters, tag countries, compute burned area (EPSG:3035)
# eu  <- load_europe_polygons()
# ba  <- read_effis_perimeters(file.path(latest, ...)) |> tag_countries(eu)

# 4) Envelope chart: 2026 daily cumulative burned area vs. past-season range
# p_envelope <- ...
# p_envelope
```

## What burned, where

*Placeholder — hero map of the current season's burn scars, monthly panels,
and country rankings will appear here as the season progresses.*
